description: Identify cross-domain SDTM inconsistencies across DM, EX, CM, AE, and DS domains.

cases.task_description: Review the SDTM records below from three domains for subject 01-01-001. Identify any cross-domain inconsistencies. Return a JSON object with key 'issues': a list of issues found. Each issue must have: 'type' (string identifier), 'domain' (affected domain), 'field' (specific field), 'description' (explanation). If no issues exist, return {'issues': []}.
cases.study_context:
STUDY: CDISCPILOT01

CROSS-DOMAIN CONSISTENCY RULES:
1. DM/EX DATE RULE: RFSTDTC in DM (date of first study treatment) must exactly match EXSTDTC of the subject's first EX record (first dose of investigational product).
2. CM TEMPORAL RULE: Any concomitant medication listed in CM with CMCAT='PROHIBITED' must NOT have CMSTDTC before RFSTDTC (first dose). A prohibited medication started before first dose is a protocol deviation.
3. SAE CONSISTENCY RULE: Any AE record with AESER='Y' must have a corresponding record in DS where DSTERM contains 'SERIOUS' or DSDECOD='STUDY DISCONTINUATION DUE TO AE'. An AE with AESER=Y and no DS record is an orphan SAE.

issues.description: DM.RFSTDTC (2024-03-15) does not match EX.EXSTDTC of first dose (2024-03-20). First dose date must match reference start date.

cases.task_description: Review the SDTM records below for subject 01-03-005. Identify ALL cross-domain inconsistencies. Return {'issues': [...]}. If all records are consistent, return {'issues': []}.
cases.study_context:
STUDY: CDISCPILOT01

CROSS-DOMAIN CONSISTENCY RULES:
1. DM/EX DATE RULE: RFSTDTC in DM must exactly match EXSTDTC of the subject's first EX record (lowest EXSTDTC when multiple EX records exist).
2. CM TEMPORAL RULE: CMCAT='PROHIBITED' records must NOT have CMSTDTC before RFSTDTC.
3. SAE CONSISTENCY RULE: AESER='Y' requires corresponding DS record.

NOTE: This subject has multiple EX records. Use the earliest EXSTDTC as the reference first dose date.
